name: CMSE 381 Exam 3 — Non-Linearity, Trees, SVM, Clustering
goal: Master ISLP Ch 7-9, 12 for CMSE 381 Midterm 3 (2026-04-20). NN/CNN topics covered separately in data_science_methods_nn domain.

history.notes: FAIL. Q1 (0.6): right intuition (coefficients linear, basis functions not) but imprecise wording — didn't cleanly state 'linear in the parameters β' or connect to OLS via design matrix. Q2 (0.5): named boundary/extrapolation blowup but missed OVERFITTING as the primary problem with degree-15 on 50 points; also didn't articulate why tails blow up (x^d dominates for large |x|, sparse leverage at edges). Q3 (0.5): correctly ID'd that x, x^2, x^3 move together so single-variable interpretation is ill-posed, but couldn't propose the alternative (partial derivative df/dx as a function of x, or f(x+1)-f(x) at a specific point). Q4 (1.0): after clarifying what 'nonlinear optimization' means, correctly said FALSE and cited closed-form solution. Re-quiz tomorrow — drill linearity-in-parameters framing, overfitting vs boundary as distinct concepts, and partial-derivative interpretation.

Q1: got param count but not full design matrix dims (n x d+1). Q2: said CI widens but missed point estimate explosion; confused extrapolation with outlier. Q3: overcomplicated linear-in-parameters — invented separation into linear/nonlinear components. Q4a correct. Q4b vague on why poly wins for smooth curves. Strong conceptual intuition, weak on precise mechanics/definitions.

Q1: dropped the top bin (x>=65) — miscounted K+1 bins. Q3: conflated piecewise linear with spline smoothness (claimed derivative continuity). Q4: only gave step-wins case, not polynomial-wins. Strong on bias/variance intuition.

Q1: still writing too many dummies (4 for 3 bins); also said betas are bin means instead of differences from baseline. Q2: right answer (85k) but wrong model form. Q3: CORRECT — smoothness ladder now solid (value continuity → piecewise linear, derivative continuity → cubic spline). Stretch Q4: decent both-sides argument. Core gap remains: K cutpoints → K+1 bins → K dummies.

Q1: correct OOB prediction (12) but computed MSE wrong (7 instead of 1) — averaged per-tree squared errors instead of squaring the averaged prediction. Q2: right spirit on LOOCV analogy but imprecise on why individual-not-grouped. Q3: applied generic LOOCV properties instead of reasoning about fewer-trees → pessimistic. Q4 interleaved step_functions: CORRECT (5 params for 4 cutpoints). Core gap: OOB calculation mechanics (average predictions first, then error).

Q1 variance math: PERFECT (σ²/B and ρσ² floor). Q2: missed bias side of pruning-vs-bagging tradeoff — said pruning reduces variance less but didnt address that pruning INCREASES bias while bagging doesnt. Q3: described random forest mechanism but didnt name it, and said "per tree" instead of "per split". Q4: right intuition on high-variance poly regression but confused OOB with bagging (OOB is estimation technique, not variance reduction). Taught out-of-order (prereqs not at mastery) due to exam proximity.

Perfect pass. Q1 all three parts correct (m=4, m=p→bagging, m=1→single random predictor). Q2 correctly separated OOB (estimation) from variance reduction (averaging + decorrelation) — directly fixed the bagging-session confusion. Q3 dominant predictor reasoning spot on. Q4 leaf mean calculation correct. Taught out-of-order (prereq decision_trees_381 still at mastery 0) but mastery transferred well.

Solid pass. Q1 dendrogram cut reading: perfect. Q2 single-linkage chaining: clean explanation. Q3 linkage distance calculations: both correct (sqrt(5) and sqrt(17)). Q4 interleaved decision tree cost function: said MSE, correct concept but imprecise — exam wants RSS terminology. Pre-question corrections needed: hierarchical is unsupervised (not classification), deterministic (no random init).
